text.title: reranking-two-stage Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of reranking-two-stage enforces.

rule.statement: First-stage pool is 2-3x final K (e.g., 50 for K=5; never < 2K).
rule.rationale: Pool too small means rerank has nothing to choose from.

rule.statement: Second stage is a cross-encoder (relevance score given (query, doc) pair); never another bi-encoder.
rule.rationale: Bi-encoder gain stage-2 over stage-1 is marginal; cross-encoder is the point.

rule.statement: Reserve 100-300ms of the latency budget for rerank; if the budget can't carry it, drop to single-stage.
rule.rationale: Squeezing rerank into 50ms is unrealistic for production cross-encoders.

rule.statement: Benchmark recall@50 (stage-1) and precision@5 (stage-2) separately; the bottleneck is whichever is weaker.
rule.rationale: Single end-to-end number hides which stage is failing.

rule.statement: If stage-2 fails, return stage-1 top-K with a degraded flag (per reranking-pipeline-integration).
Skip when recall@50 is too low to benefit from rerank or when latency budget cannot carry the second stage.
rule.rationale: Stub rule referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml when none of the qualifying branches fire.
